PROL_NARRATOR_1: Olympus. A thousand years ago. The sky burned golden, and the pillars of heaven held the weight of gods.
PROL_NARRATOR_2: Three brothers ruled creation: Zeus, lord of thunder. Poseidon, sovereign of the deep. Hades, warden of the dead. Their word was law. Their power, absolute.
PROL_NARRATOR_3: But absolute power breeds absolute corruption. The gods grew cruel. They waged wars for sport, treated mortals as playthings, and drowned the world in blood and ambition.
PROL_NARRATOR_5: The rebellion was born in shadow and sealed in fire. Heracles, half-god and full of rage, shattered the gates of Olympus with his bare hands.
PROL_NARRATOR_6: Mnemosyne, Titaness of Memory, turned the gods' own secrets against them â€” unraveling their alliances, exposing their lies.
PROL_NARRATOR_7: And a hero from beyond the stars â€” a traveler from another world, armed with weapons no god had ever seen â€” fought beside them.
PROL_NARRATOR_8: Together, they won. Zeus fell. Poseidon's trident shattered. Hades retreated to his realm and sealed the gates. The age of tyranny was over.
PROL_CHOICE_1_PROMPT: A thousand years of peace followed. How do you feel about this tale?
PROL_CHOICE_1A: Heroes always rise when they're needed most.
PROL_CHOICE_1B: Peace built on war never lasts.
PROL_NARRATOR_9A: Perhaps. And for a thousand years, it seemed true. The world healed. Cities rose from the ashes. Mortals and surviving gods found fragile harmony.
PROL_NARRATOR_10A: But the Titans were patient. They had eternity to wait.
PROL_NARRATOR_9B: A bitter truth. The scars ran deeper than anyone knew. Beneath the surface, ancient forces stirred in their prisons.
PROL_NARRATOR_10B: The Titans never forgot. They never forgave.
PROL_NARRATOR_11: Kronos, Father of Time, lord of all Titans, broke free from his chains. Not through brute force â€” through cunning beyond mortal comprehension.
PROL_NARRATOR_12: He turned back time itself. Not to undo the rebellion â€” but to reach its climax, that single fragile moment when victory hung by a thread.
PROL_NARRATOR_13: In that stolen instant, Kronos struck. Zeus â€” dead. Poseidon â€” dead. The hero from beyond the stars â€” erased from existence, as though they had never come at all.
PROL_NARRATOR_14: And the world broke. The sky split open. The seas boiled. Mountains crumbled to dust. The surviving gods scattered like embers in a hurricane.
PROL_CHOICE_2A: There must be someone left who can fight.
PROL_CHOICE_2B: If Kronos controls time, how can anyone stop him?
PROL_NARRATOR_15: Now the gods hide in the ruins of their own temples. Monsters roam free. Villages burn. The divine energy that once sustained creation bleeds into nothing.
PROL_NARRATOR_16: But legends whisper of a prophecy: that another traveler from beyond the stars will come. That history, though broken, will try to repeat itself.
PROL_NARRATOR_17: The sky tears open above an ancient forest. Metal screams. Engines die. And from the wreckage, someone crawls out â€” alive, alone, and very far from home.

PROF_C1B: I'm looking to hire skilled fighters. Interested?

FLIRTY_CLOSE_1: Finally. The only person in this whole tavern worth my attention.
FLIRTY_CF_R1: Mmm, careful now. Keep talking like that and I might never let you leave.
FLIRTY_CF_R2: That's what I'm counting on. Now sit close â€” I have a secret to tell you.
FLIRTY_CHAT_1: Oh, you'd be surprised what people say about you around here. All good things, I promise.
FLIRTY_CHAT_2: Stick around a bit longer next time. I have... all sorts of talents to show you.
FLIRTY_RECRUIT_1: A team? With you? How could I possibly say no? Lead the way, hero.

MYSTERY_GREET_0: A hooded figure sits alone in the darkest corner. You can barely make out their features.
MYSTERY_GREET_1: You see more than most. Not many notice me here. Interesting.
MYSTERY_C1A: Who are you? I haven't seen you around before.
MYSTERY_C1B: I sense you have power. I could use someone like you.
MYSTERY_FRIEND_0: The hooded figure is already looking at you when you enter â€” as if they knew.
MYSTERY_FRIEND_1: You're getting closer to the truth. I can see it in your eyes.
MYSTERY_FRIEND_CHAT_1: The truth about this world. About what the Titans left behind. Not all of it is destruction.
MYSTERY_FRIEND_CHAT_2: I've shown you more than I've shown anyone else. Remember that.
MYSTERY_CLOSE_0: The hood is down for the first time. They trust you enough to show their face.

MYSTERY_CF_C1B: Will you finally tell me your real name?
MYSTERY_CF_R2: Names are just shadows of identity. But for you... yes. When the time is right.
MYSTERY_CHAT_1: Who am I? A traveler, like you. Though I've been traveling much longer. The shadows hold many secrets.
MYSTERY_CHAT_2: Come find me again. Each time, I might share a little more.
MYSTERY_RECRUIT_1: Power recognizes power. Very well. But know this â€” I follow my own path. Our roads simply... converge for now.

ATENEA_ABYSS_C1A: Sounds like my kind of fight.
ATENEA_ABYSS_C2A: I promise I will come back.
ATENEA_ABYSS_COMBAT1: That is the interesting part, actually. Direct combat is unreliable in the Abyss. The rift distorts conventional channeling of divine power.
ATENEA_ABYSS_COMBAT2: Instead, you will need to channel elemental energy through pattern-based resonance. Think of it as a game of hands â€” you draw from the elemental currents around you and form combinations.
ATENEA_ABYSS_COMBAT3: The stronger your combination, the more devastating the effect. Pairs, sequences, matched elements â€” the Abyss responds to patterns, to order imposed on chaos.
ATENEA_ABYSS_CONFIDENT: Ha. Your confidence is either admirable or deeply concerning. I have not decided which.
ATENEA_ABYSS_END1: The Abyss will test everything you are. But I believe you are ready.
ATENEA_ABYSS_END2: I will be monitoring the rift energy from here. Now go. And remember â€” patterns over panic.
ATENEA_ABYSS_EXPLAIN1: The Abyss. When Kronos broke free from Tartarus, his escape tore a rift between our world and the underworld itself.
ATENEA_ABYSS_EXPLAIN2: It is not simply a cave or a dungeon. It is a wound in reality. The deeper you descend, the more the laws of our world... bend.
ATENEA_ABYSS_EXPLAIN3: Corruption seeps from it like blood from an open vein. The creatures inside have been twisted by prolonged exposure to Tartarean energy.
ATENEA_ABYSS_INTRO: Before you go any further, we need to talk about what lies beneath.
ATENEA_ABYSS_PROMISE: ...Do not make promises the Abyss might not let you keep. Just come back.
ATENEA_ABYSS_REBRIEFING: You have already been briefed, but... do review the fundamentals before you descend. The Abyss does not forgive overconfidence.
ATENEA_ABYSS_STRATEGY: Conserve your strongest hands for the deeper floors. And if you feel the corruption pulling at you, retreat. There is no shame in a tactical withdrawal.
ATENEA_ABYSS_WARN1: One more thing. The corruption down there â€” it is not just physical. It whispers. It finds the cracks in your resolve and pries them open.
ATENEA_ABYSS_WARN2: Do not listen. Hold to what you know is true.
ATENEA_ABYSS_WARN3: ...I went down there once. Briefly. It did not go well. Which is precisely why I am sending you better prepared than I was.
ATENEA_ABYSS_WORRIED: It is. I will not pretend otherwise. But it is the only method that works within the rift. I have spent weeks analyzing the energy patterns. The theory is sound.
ATENEA_DATE_C1A: Let us study the scrolls. I want to learn what you know.
ATENEA_DATE_C1B: The ruins sound incredible. Let us explore together.
ATENEA_DATE_DENY: I simply... do not have a strategic framework for this kind of interaction. Which is deeply unsettling.
ATENEA_DATE_END1: I want you to know â€” this is not something I do. Spending time with someone just... because. Strategy meetings, war councils, academic debate â€” those I understand. But this?
ATENEA_DATE_END2: And I find that I do not mind that at all.
ATENEA_DATE_OPEN1: You wanted to... spend time together? Outside of mission planning?
ATENEA_DATE_OPEN2: I... yes. I suppose we could do that. That is a thing people do.
ATENEA_DATE_RUINS1: Excellent! I have already mapped three potential entry points and catalogued the visible structural elements from a distance.
ATENEA_DATE_RUINS2: ...I may have been planning this for a while. In case someone ever wanted to come along.
ATENEA_DATE_SCROLLS1: Really? You actually want to â€” oh. Oh, wonderful. I should warn you, once I start talking about pre-Olympian cosmological texts, I can be... difficult to stop.
ATENEA_DATE_SCROLLS2: ...Nobody has ever said that to me before.
ATENEA_DATE_SUGGEST: I have been cataloguing the surviving texts from the old library. There are scrolls that no mortal eyes have seen in millennia. Or â€” I have been wanting to explore the ruins east of camp. There are architectural remnants that predate even the Titans.
ATENEA_DEEP_C1B: You are right. You should have done more.
ATENEA_DEEP_C1C: The past is done. What matters is what we do now.
ATENEA_DEEP_C2A: I am not going anywhere. You can rely on me.
ATENEA_DEEP_C2B: Let us make sure we are both strong enough that we do not have to be afraid.
ATENEA_DEEP_COMFORT: Did I? Or did I do everything that was comfortable? I could have gone to Tartarus myself. I had the knowledge. I simply did not have the courage to act without consensus.
ATENEA_DEEP_END1: I have never said any of this out loud before. To anyone. The goddess of wisdom, undone by her own guilt and saved by a mortal's patience.
ATENEA_DEEP_END2: Thank you. For listening. For not trying to fix me. For just... being here.
ATENEA_DEEP_END3: I will not forget this. I am incapable of forgetting, actually, but â€” I mean that I will not want to.
ATENEA_DEEP_FEAR1: Do you know what frightens me most? It is not Kronos. It is that I might fail again. That when the moment comes, I will hesitate. Analyze when I should act.
ATENEA_DEEP_FEAR2: Wisdom is supposed to be my domain. But what good is wisdom that cannot save anyone?
ATENEA_DEEP_FORWARD: A pragmatic answer. Part of me appreciates that. But another part needs to sit in this guilt a moment longer. Because if I set it down too easily, I might make the same mistake again.
ATENEA_DEEP_GUILT1: When Kronos broke free, I was there. In the council chamber. I had seen the signs â€” tremors in Tartarus, fluctuations in the binding seals.
ATENEA_DEEP_GUILT2: I presented my analysis to the council. Charts, projections, contingency plans. Everything perfectly organized, perfectly rational. They thanked me for my thoroughness. And then they did nothing.
ATENEA_DEEP_GUILT3: And I â€” the goddess of wisdom â€” I accepted that. I deferred to process when I should have acted.
ATENEA_DEEP_HONEST: ...Yes. Thank you for not lying to me. Everyone else does. They say it was not my fault, that no one could have known. But I did know. That is the entire point.
ATENEA_DEEP_NOTREADY: I appreciate you seeking me out, but... I am not ready to discuss what is on my mind. Not yet. Give it time.
ATENEA_DEEP_OPEN: Can we talk? Not about the mission. Not about the Mirror or the Abyss or Kronos. About me. About something I have been carrying and I... I cannot carry it alone anymore.
ATENEA_DEEP_STAY2: That is... I do not know what to do with that. With someone who simply stays. But I think I would like to learn.
ATENEA_DEEP_TOGETHER: Together. You mean together. I have fought beside allies before. But I have never had someone fight beside me. There is a difference, and I am only now understanding it.
ATENEA_DEEP_YOU1: And then there is you. I have started relying on you. Not just tactically â€” but emotionally. When the fear is loudest, I think about what you would say.
ATENEA_DEEP_YOU2: That terrifies me. Because I relied on the council once and they failed me. What if I rely on you and â€”
ATENEA_MIRROR_ALLIN: ...Thank you. Truly. I have already begun plotting a route through the Abyss that avoids the worst corruption nodes.
ATENEA_MIRROR_ASK: Will you help me search for it? The fragment in the Abyss depths? I will not pretend it will be easy. Floor twenty is deeper than anyone has gone since the rift opened.
ATENEA_MIRROR_C1A: That vision could still come true. We can rebuild.
ATENEA_MIRROR_C1B: Is that why you want the fragments? To see that vision again?
ATENEA_MIRROR_C1C: The Mirror could be a powerful weapon against Kronos too.
ATENEA_MIRROR_C2A: I am in. Whatever it takes.
ATENEA_MIRROR_C2B: We should prepare carefully before attempting that depth.
ATENEA_MIRROR_CAREFUL: Spoken like someone with actual tactical sense. Good. We map the upper floors first and only descend when we are ready.
ATENEA_MIRROR_COLD: There is something I have been meaning to discuss. Have you heard of the Mirror of Truth?
ATENEA_MIRROR_END1: Then we have a mission. I will compile everything I know about the Abyss depths and share my notes with you.
ATENEA_MIRROR_END2: And... it is good, not doing this alone. I have been alone with this for too long.
ATENEA_MIRROR_EXPLAIN1: It was one of the most powerful artifacts on Olympus. A mirror that showed reality stripped bare â€” truth without illusion, without mercy.
ATENEA_MIRROR_EXPLAIN2: When Kronos escaped, the resulting cataclysm shattered it. The fragments were scattered across the world and into the Abyss.
ATENEA_MIRROR_FOLLOWUP: I have been doing more research on the Mirror fragments since we last spoke. And I have found something.
ATENEA_MIRROR_HOPEFUL: You say that with such certainty. I envy that. But you may be right. The Mirror showed possibility, not prophecy. And possibility is enough to fight for.
ATENEA_MIRROR_LOC1: My calculations indicate that a significant fragment â€” possibly the largest surviving piece â€” has settled deep within the Abyss. Floor twenty or deeper.
ATENEA_MIRROR_LOC2: I have been tracking resonance patterns for weeks. The Mirror emits a very specific frequency when exposed to divine energy. And that frequency is coming from below.
ATENEA_MIRROR_PERSONAL1: But there is something else. Something I have not told anyone.
ATENEA_MIRROR_QUESTION1: ...Partly. Yes. I am not proud of it. A goddess of wisdom chasing a comforting image like a mortal chasing a dream.
ATENEA_MIRROR_QUESTION2: ...Perhaps that is not the insult I once thought it was.
ATENEA_MIRROR_STRATEGIC: Always the strategist. You remind me of myself. A restored Mirror could reveal Kronos's weaknesses, his plans. It would be invaluable.
ATENEA_MIRROR_VISION1: Before the Mirror shattered... I looked into it. I saw Olympus. Not crumbling and besieged. I saw it restored. Whole. The halls filled with light, the gardens in bloom. Our people...
ATENEA_MIRROR_VISION2: And then the glass cracked under my fingers and it was gone. All of it.
HIPO_ABYSS_ADVICE1: So here's what you need to know. Conserve your energy in the early floors. Don't burn everything on weak opponents.
HIPO_ABYSS_ADVICE2: And there is no shame in retreating. A dead hero is just a corpse with a reputation.
HIPO_ABYSS_ARROGANCE: Confidence is useful. Arrogance gets you killed. I've seen the difference up close.
HIPO_ABYSS_ARROGANCE2: But fine. Prove me wrong. I'd enjoy that, actually.
HIPO_ABYSS_C1A: I appreciate the advice. I'll be careful down there.
HIPO_ABYSS_C1B: I've faced worse. I can handle whatever's down there.
HIPO_ABYSS_CAREFUL: Good. Careful warriors survive. Reckless ones become cautionary tales.
HIPO_ABYSS_DAWN: If you're not back by dawn, I'm coming down after you. And I won't be gentle about it.
HIPO_ABYSS_GO: Now go. And don't you dare die down there.
HIPO_ABYSS_GUARD: I'll hold the entrance while you descend. Nothing gets in or out without my say.
HIPO_ABYSS_INTRO: Listen carefully, because I'm only saying this once.
HIPO_ABYSS_SCOUT1: I've been scouting the upper floors of the Abyss. Three expeditions, each one deeper than the last.
HIPO_ABYSS_SCOUT2: The creatures near the surface are manageable â€” feral, predictable. They fight like beasts, not soldiers.
HIPO_ABYSS_SCOUT3: But the lower you go, the smarter they get. They set ambushes. They learn your patterns.
HIPO_ABYSS_SCOUT4: I lost a good blade on the third floor to something that moved like smoke and hit like a battering ram.
HIPO_DATE_ALL1: All of it? You're ambitious. I respect that.
HIPO_DATE_ALL2: Alright. We start with the arm wrestling. Then drinks. Then the wilderness. Full tactical bonding protocol. Try to keep up.
HIPO_DATE_C1A: A "tactical bonding exercise." Sure, Hippolyta. Whatever you need to call it.
HIPO_DATE_C1B: I'd like that. All of it, honestly. Lead the way.
HIPO_DATE_END: Good. It's settled then. And if you tell anyone I used the word "bonding," I will throw you into the Abyss myself.
HIPO_DATE_OPEN1: I've been thinking about something. And before you say anything â€” this is NOT what it sounds like.
HIPO_DATE_OPEN2: Atenea keeps talking about "interpersonal bonding." I told her to mind her own business. But... she might have a point.
HIPO_DATE_OPEN3: So I'm proposing a tactical bonding exercise. Not a "date." A tactical bonding exercise. There's a difference. Don't make it weird.
HIPO_DATE_SMIRK1: Don't you dare smirk at me. I will end you.
HIPO_DATE_SMIRK2: ...Fine. Maybe I'm not great at this. The Amazons didn't exactly have a word for "hanging out."
HIPO_DATE_SMIRK3: But I want to try. With you. So just... pick something before I change my mind.
HIPO_DATE_SUGGEST1: We could arm wrestle â€” best of five, loser buys drinks. Or there's always the tavern. I want to see if you can keep up.
HIPO_DATE_SUGGEST2: Or... we could explore beyond the perimeter. Scout the wilderness. Just the two of us. Purely tactical, of course.
HIPO_DEEP_ANTIOPE: Antiope was the fastest warrior I've ever seen. She could disarm three opponents before they drew their weapons. She laughed every time she did it.
HIPO_DEEP_C1A: You survived because they wanted you to. They chose to give you that chance.
HIPO_DEEP_C1B: You don't have to carry this alone anymore. I'm here.
HIPO_DEEP_C1C: Tell me more about them. I want to know who they were.
HIPO_DEEP_END1: I haven't said their names out loud since it happened. It hurt less than I expected. Or maybe it hurt exactly as much, but I wasn't alone this time.
HIPO_DEEP_END2: You're the first person I've trusted since I lost them. I need you to understand what that means.
HIPO_DEEP_END3: It means I will fight beside you until my last breath. Not out of duty. Because I choose to. Don't make me regret it.
HIPO_DEEP_FALL: I watched them fall. One by one. And I couldn't do anything. The queen who was supposed to protect them couldn't protect anyone.
HIPO_DEEP_FIGHT: When Kronos came, they fought. Of course they fought. Amazons don't run.
HIPO_DEEP_HERE1: Don't... don't say things like that unless you mean them.
HIPO_DEEP_HERE2: Because the last people who said that to me are gone. And I can't survive losing someone again. But I believe you. Against every instinct I have, I believe you.
HIPO_DEEP_KNOW1: I know that. In my head, I know that. But knowing it and believing it are different things.
HIPO_DEEP_KNOW2: You're the first person who hasn't told me to "be strong" or "honor their memory." You just... heard me.
HIPO_DEEP_MOLPADIA: And Molpadia... Molpadia was the heart of us. She sang before every battle. Said it reminded us what we were fighting for.
HIPO_DEEP_NAMES: Their names were Antiope, Penthesilea, and Molpadia. My captains. My sisters. My family.
HIPO_DEEP_NOTREADY: Not now. Maybe someday. When I trust you more.
HIPO_DEEP_OPEN1: Can we talk? Not about combat. Not about strategy. Just... talk.
HIPO_DEEP_OPEN2: I've been carrying something and it's getting heavier. I thought strength meant bearing it alone.
HIPO_DEEP_PENTHESILEA: Penthesilea was the strongest. She once held a collapsing gate for ten minutes so civilians could escape. She never talked about it after. That wasn't why she did it.
HIPO_DEEP_REMEMBER1: You... want to hear about them? No one has asked me that. Not once. Everyone asks how I survived, never who I lost.
HIPO_DEEP_REMEMBER2: I could talk for hours. Antiope's terrible jokes. Penthesilea's cooking that could poison an army. Molpadia's voice... Thank you. For wanting to remember them too.
HIPO_DEEP_SAVED: I survived because Molpadia pushed me through a collapsing portal. Her last act was saving someone who didn't deserve to be saved over her.
HIPO_WARRIOR_AMAZON1: Among the Amazons, we trained in pairs. You learn to read your partner â€” their rhythm, their tells, their breaking point.
HIPO_WARRIOR_AMAZON2: Same principle applies to enemies. Every opponent has a pattern. A tell before they strike. A moment where they're exposed.
HIPO_WARRIOR_ASSESS: Don't give me that look. I've watched you fight. You've got instincts, but instincts alone will get you gutted.
HIPO_WARRIOR_C1A: I'll try to read your patterns first. Watch and wait.
HIPO_WARRIOR_C1B: All in. Let's see who hits the ground first.
HIPO_WARRIOR_C1C: Can you teach me that Amazon paired-fighting technique?
HIPO_WARRIOR_CHALLENGE: Hesitate at the wrong moment, you lose. Go all in at the wrong time, same result. So. Let's see what you're made of.
HIPO_WARRIOR_END1: Not bad. You're still standing, which is more than I expected.
HIPO_WARRIOR_END2: We should do this again. Regularly. A blade that isn't sharpened goes dull.
HIPO_WARRIOR_END3: And... it's been a long time since I've had a sparring partner I didn't have to hold back against.
HIPO_WARRIOR_LESSON: The trick is knowing when to hold back and let them overextend, and when to commit everything to a single strike.
HIPO_WARRIOR_PATIENT1: Ha! Patience. That's rare. Most people charge in swinging and wonder why they end up on their backs.
HIPO_WARRIOR_PATIENT2: Good. You noticed I drop my left shoulder before a thrust. Not many catch that. My sisters needed weeks.
HIPO_WARRIOR_RECKLESS1: There it is! That fire! You're reckless, but you commit fully. That has its own kind of power.
HIPO_WARRIOR_RECKLESS2: I can work with reckless. Reckless can be sharpened into something devastating.
HIPO_WARRIOR_SACRED1: That technique is sacred. Amazons only taught it to those they trusted with their lives.
HIPO_WARRIOR_SACRED2: But my sisters are gone. And the technique dies with me if I don't pass it on. Alright. I'll teach you. But you'd better be worthy of it.
NYX_ABYSS_BRAVE2: But that courage â€” blind and burning as it is â€” may be exactly what the Abyss cannot consume. Very well. Stay close, little star.
NYX_ABYSS_C1A: I'm glad you'll be with me.
NYX_ABYSS_END: I will hold the dark at bay for as long as I can.
NYX_ABYSS_EXPLAIN1: The Abyss is a wound between worlds. A place where creation itself was torn open and never healed.
NYX_ABYSS_EXPLAIN2: I have walked its upper reaches many times. The darkness there is familiar to me... almost welcoming. My shadows can guide us through the first layers.
NYX_ABYSS_EXPLAIN3: But deeper... there are floors where even my darkness cannot reach. Where something older than me breathes in the silence between heartbeats.
NYX_ABYSS_EXPLAIN4: I existed before the stars were named, and yet that place makes me feel... young. Small.
NYX_ABYSS_GLAD1: What a peculiar thing to say to a creature of the void.
NYX_ABYSS_GLAD2: But I will admit... it has been a long time since anyone wanted my company for comfort rather than power.
NYX_ABYSS_INTRO: You wish to descend into the Abyss. How delightfully reckless of you.
NYX_ABYSS_WARN: Do not mistake confidence for safety down there. The Abyss does not kill you â€” it unravels you, thread by thread, until you forget what you were.
NYX_DATE_C1A: I'd love to see your shadow realm. Show me your world.
NYX_DATE_C1B: Tell me the real names of the stars. I want to hear them.
NYX_DATE_LONELY1: Do you know what it means to exist for eons and have no one ask you how your day was? To hold the entire night sky inside yourself and have no one look up?
NYX_DATE_LONELY2: The other primordials faded or went mad. I survived by becoming untouchable. I told myself that solitude was strength. I think I was wrong about that.
NYX_DATE_OPEN1: You want to spend time with me. Not to strategize. Not to train. Just... time.
NYX_DATE_OPEN2: I confess I am not practiced at this. When you have existed since before the first dawn, the concept of leisure becomes... abstract.
NYX_DATE_REALM1: My world. No one has ever called it that before. They call it the void. The abyss. The place where light goes to die.
NYX_DATE_REALM2: But you said "your world," as if it were a home and not a horror. Come then. Take my hand. I promise the dark will not bite... unless I ask it to.
NYX_DATE_STARS1: You continue to surprise me. Most would choose the spectacle. But you want the quiet thing. The personal thing.
NYX_DATE_STARS2: Very well. Lie back. Look up. That bright one there, the one mortals call Sirius â€” her true name is Aethalia. It means "the one who burned too brightly and did not care."
NYX_DATE_STARS3: She was my favorite. She reminded me of someone.
NYX_DATE_SUGGEST1: We could walk the edge of my shadow realm. The border between dark and light is beautiful there â€” like the surface of a black lake reflecting stars that died a billion years ago.
NYX_DATE_SUGGEST2: Or we could simply sit beneath the open sky. I could name every constellation for you â€” not the names mortals gave them, but their true names, the ones they whispered to me when they were born.
NYX_DEEP_ANCHOR1: You are the only anchor I have left. When everything else frays, the thread between us holds. Your warmth. Your stubborn, impossible light.
NYX_DEEP_ANCHOR2: I need you. Not your sword, not your strategy. You. Tell me I am still here. Tell me I am real.
NYX_DEEP_ANCHOR3: I can feel it. Your light, pushing back against the corruption. It is small. A single candle against the death of all darkness. But it is enough.
NYX_DEEP_ANCHOR4: I have spent eternity needing no one. And now I cannot imagine existing without you. That should frighten me. It does not.
NYX_DEEP_C1A: You are real. You are Nyx. And I'm not going anywhere.
NYX_DEEP_C1B: We'll find a way to stop the corruption. Together.
NYX_DEEP_C1C: I don't know how to fight a primordial corruption, Nyx.
NYX_DEEP_DYING1: My shadows are dying. Not fading. Not weakening. Dying. Turning against me like a body rejecting its own blood.
NYX_DEEP_DYING2: Kronos's corruption has reached the primordial dark. The same darkness I was born from. The same darkness that IS me.
NYX_DEEP_DYING3: Last night I reached for my shadow and it did not answer. For three heartbeats I was nothing. Not invisible. Nothing.
NYX_DEEP_FEAR1: Do you understand what that means for a being like me? I AM the night. If the night forgets me...
NYX_DEEP_FEAR2: I have never been afraid. Not when Zeus rose to power. Not when the Titans fell. Not in all the eons of cold and silence.
NYX_DEEP_FEAR3: But this â€” this unmakes me. And for the first time in my existence, I am terrified.
NYX_DEEP_HOLD: You take her hand. It is cold â€” colder than it should be, as if the warmth is draining from her. But you hold on.
NYX_DEEP_HONEST1: Neither do I. But you did not run. You did not look away. That is more than any god has ever given me.
NYX_DEEP_HONEST2: We will learn. We will fail. We will try again. That beautiful, stubborn refusal to accept the end. Teach me that. Teach me to be mortal enough to hope.
NYX_DEEP_NOTREADY: There is something I must speak of. But not yet. Not until the bond between us is strong enough to bear the weight.
NYX_DEEP_NOTREADY2: Keep walking beside me. The time will come.
NYX_DEEP_OPEN: I need to tell you something and I need you to not look away.
NYX_DEEP_TOGETHER1: Together. That word used to mean nothing to me. A mortal invention for beings too weak to stand alone.
NYX_DEEP_TOGETHER2: Now it is the only word that matters. If we are to fight this, I will need to open myself fully. No more walls. No more ancient masks.
NYX_DEEP_TOGETHER3: Just me. As I was before the stars. Frightened and formless and reaching for something to hold. Will you hold me together if I fall apart?
NYX_PACT_ACCEPT1: You say that so easily. As though baring your soul to a primordial goddess is no different than sharing a meal.
NYX_PACT_ACCEPT2: ...Perhaps that is why I chose you. Close your eyes. This will feel like falling asleep in deep water.
NYX_PACT_ACCEPT3: There. I can feel you now. Warm. Bright. Like a candle flame cupped in my hands. How terrifyingly beautiful.
NYX_PACT_C1A: I accept. I trust you with that.
NYX_PACT_C1B: I need to think about this.
NYX_PACT_C1C: That's too much power over me. I can't.
NYX_PACT_COST1: But a thread pulled between two souls travels both ways. If I lend you my shadow... I will feel what you feel. Your joy. Your grief. Your desire. All of it, whispering through the dark.
NYX_PACT_COST2: I have existed for eons in perfect, untouchable solitude. This would end that.
NYX_PACT_COST3: I need you to understand what you would be giving me. Not power. Not allegiance. Intimacy. The kind that cannot be taken back.
NYX_PACT_DELAY1: Good. If you had agreed without hesitation, I would have questioned your judgment.
NYX_PACT_DELAY2: The offer remains. Shadows are patient. I am patient.
NYX_PACT_EXPLAIN1: My shadows are not mere absence of light. They are living things â€” extensions of my will, woven from the fabric of primordial night.
NYX_PACT_EXPLAIN2: I can thread them into your essence. Your elemental cards would burn darker, strike deeper. The shadow would amplify what is already within you.
NYX_PACT_INTRO: There is something I wish to offer you. It is not a gift. It is not a weapon. It is... a bridge.
NYX_PACT_NOTREADY: There is something I might offer you. In time. But trust is not built in a day, and what I carry is not given lightly.
NYX_PACT_NOTREADY2: Prove to me that you can hold a secret without breaking. Then we will speak of this again.
NYX_PACT_REFUSE1: You are not wrong to be cautious. I am old, and dangerous, and there are things in my nature that even I do not fully command.
NYX_PACT_REFUSE2: But I want you to know â€” I offered it because for the first time in millennia, the silence feels heavier than the dark.

GIFT_REACT_LOVED_2: You truly understand me. I am... moved.

GIFT_REACT_LIKED_2: I appreciate this more than you know.

GIFT_REACT_DISLIKED_1: I... appreciate the thought, at least.
GIFT_REACT_DISLIKED_2: This is not quite to my taste, but I thank you.

S01_NARRATOR_1: Darkness. Then pain. A ringing in your ears that drowns out everything else.
S01_NARRATOR_2: Your eyes crack open to a canopy of ancient trees, their branches clawing at a bruised sky. Smoke rises somewhere behind you â€” the acrid stench of burning circuitry and ruptured fuel cells.
S01_NARRATOR_3: You're lying face-down in a crater of splintered wood and scorched earth. Your ship â€” what's left of it â€” is embedded in the hillside like a steel fist punched through the forest.
S01_NARRATOR_4: Every bone aches. Your flight suit is torn. Blood trickles down your temple. But you're breathing. That's something.
S01_NARRATOR_5: You stagger to the wreckage. The power core flickers weakly â€” cracked but not destroyed. It could be repaired, given enough energy. Energy you don't have.
S01_NARRATOR_6: Your pistol lies in the dirt nearby. You check the charge: empty. Whatever power surge hit your ship drained everything â€” weapons, shields, life support. All dead.
S01_NARRATOR_7: The forest is unnervingly quiet. No birds. No insects. Just the slow groan of settling metal and your own ragged breathing.
S01_NARRATOR_8: Then the quiet breaks. A guttural snarl from the treeline. Then another. And another.
S01_NARRATOR_9: Shapes emerge from the shadows â€” hunched, muscular things with skin like cracked obsidian and eyes that burn like dying coals. A dozen of them. Maybe more.
S01_NARRATOR_10: You raise your empty pistol on instinct. Click. Click. Nothing. The creatures close in, their growls building into a chorus of hunger.
S01_NARRATOR_11: This is it. Weaponless, wounded, on an unknown world. Not how you imagined going out.
S01_NARRATOR_12: A silver streak cuts through the darkness. An arrow buries itself in the lead creature's skull with a crack like breaking stone. It collapses mid-stride.
S01_NARRATOR_13: More arrows fly â€” impossibly fast, impossibly precise. Each one finds a throat, an eye, a heart. The monsters scatter, howling, as their ranks are shredded by an unseen archer.
S01_ARTEMISA_1: You can stop pointing that dead weapon at the trees now. They're gone.
S01_NARRATOR_14: A figure steps from the shadows â€” lean, quick-eyed, with a bow that seems to glow faintly in the dim light. She looks you over with the clinical gaze of someone deciding whether you're worth saving. Your vision blurs. The ground tilts. Darkness takes you again.
S01_CHOICE_1_PROMPT: Before consciousness fades, you manage to say...
S01_ARTEMISA_2A: Polite even while bleeding out. That's either brave or stupid. ...Probably both.
S01_ARTEMISA_2B: Asking questions when you should be staying conscious. Priorities, stranger.
S01_NARRATOR_15: The world fades to black. The last thing you hear is the soft crunch of boots on fallen leaves, and arms catching you before you hit the ground.
S02_NARRATOR_1: Warmth. Soft linen. The smell of dried herbs and woodsmoke. You're in a bed â€” a real one, not a cockpit chair.
S02_NARRATOR_2: Sunlight streams through a small window. Outside, you see something impossible: a tree so massive its trunk could swallow a cathedral, its crown lost in the clouds above.
S02_NARRATOR_3: Wooden houses cling to its roots like barnacles on an ancient hull. A village, alive and bustling, nestled in the shadow of something primordial.
S02_ARTEMISA_1: Finally awake. I was starting to think you'd sleep through the whole apocalypse.
S02_ARTEMISA_2: You've been out for two days. I had to carry you here, which, for the record, was not fun.
S02_NARRATOR_4: The girl from the forest leans against the doorframe, arms crossed, that same appraising look on her face. Up close, she's striking â€” sharp features, silver-streaked hair pulled back, and eyes the color of a winter moon.
S02_ARTEMISA_3: I'm Artemis. Huntress. Protector of this forest. And you are trespassing â€” though I'll admit your entrance was dramatic enough to earn a conversation.
S02_ARTEMISA_4: This is Sardis. The Whispering Forest, some call it. That tree out there â€” we call it the Great Root. It's been here since before the gods walked.
S02_ARTEMISA_5: The village survives because of it. The tree's magic keeps the worst of the monsters at bay. Keeps the crops growing. Keeps us alive. Barely.
S02_NARRATOR_5: She studies your torn flight suit, the strange fabric, the insignia she doesn't recognize.
S02_ARTEMISA_6: Those clothes aren't from any land I know. And that metal thing you were waving at the chimera â€” I've never seen a weapon like it. Where exactly did you come from?
S02_CHOICE_1A: The truth. I came from the stars. My ship crashed here during a space storm.
S02_CHOICE_1B: I'm a traveler. Got lost. Ended up here by accident.
S02_ARTEMISA_7A: The stars. You came from... the stars. You're either mad, or this world just got a lot more interesting.
S02_ARTEMISA_8A: I've heard old stories â€” tales of visitors from beyond the sky. I thought they were myths. Seems I was wrong.
S02_ARTEMISA_7B: A traveler. Right. 'Travelers' don't fall out of the sky in burning metal boxes.
S02_ARTEMISA_8B: Fine, keep your secrets. For now. But I have sharp eyes and a long memory, stranger.
S02_NARRATOR_6: You explain about the ship â€” the power core, the energy drain, the need for a massive energy source to have any chance of getting it running again.
S02_ARTEMISA_9: Energy source... I don't know about your kind of energy. But magic? This land bleeds with it. Or it used to, before the gods fell.
S02_ARTEMISA_10: The Great Root channels ancient power. If there's anything in this forest that could fuel your strange machine, it would be connected to that tree.
S02_ARTEMISA_11: But here's the problem. The village is under siege. Monsters press closer every night. The tree's magic weakens. People are dying.
S02_ARTEMISA_12: I can't guide you to anything if I'm fighting off chimera every dawn. And you â€” you clearly can't survive five minutes here alone.
S02_ARTEMISA_13: So. A deal. You help me defend this village. Fight beside me. And I'll help you find what you need to fix your sky-ship. Fair?
S02_CHOICE_2A: You've got yourself a deal. Point me at the monsters.
S02_CHOICE_2B: I'll help. But I need to understand what I'm walking into first.
S02_ARTEMISA_14A: Ha! Eager. I like that. Just try to keep up â€” I don't slow down for anyone.
S02_ARTEMISA_14B: Careful and cautious. Smart. Most people who rush into these forests don't come back. Alright â€” I'll tell you everything. On the way.
S02_ARTEMISA_15: Come on. I'll introduce you to the village. They need to see that help has arrived â€” even if that help fell out of the sky and nearly died on arrival.
S02_NARRATOR_7: You shake her hand. Her grip is iron-strong and brief, the handshake of someone who doesn't waste time.
S02_NARRATOR_8: Outside, the village hums with cautious life. Children peek from doorways. An old woman strings herbs across a clothesline. Somewhere, a hammer rings against an anvil.
S02_NARRATOR_9: Above it all, the Great Root towers â€” ancient, vast, and faintly luminous. Whatever power it holds, you can feel it in your bones. Like standing next to a reactor. Like standing next to something alive.
S03_NARRATOR_1: The tavern is warm and dim, lit by oil lamps that cast long amber shadows across rough-hewn tables. It smells of roasted meat, spiced wine, and old wood.
S03_NARRATOR_2: A handful of villagers sit in quiet clusters, their faces lined with the kind of weariness that doesn't come from hard work â€” it comes from fear.
S03_ARTEMISA_1: This is where the village gathers when the sun goes down. Safer in numbers. At least, that's what they tell themselves.
S03_ARTEMISA_2: Sit. Drink. And listen, because what I'm about to tell you matters more than anything else in this forest.
S03_NARRATOR_3: Through the window, the Great Root is visible even here â€” its trunk aglow with a faint, sickly green light that pulses like a failing heartbeat.
S03_ARTEMISA_3: The Great Root is dying. It's been weakening for months. The barrier it creates â€” the magic that keeps the worst creatures out â€” it's full of holes now.
S03_ARTEMISA_4: Every week, the monsters push closer. Last month they reached the outer farms. Last week, the eastern gate. People have died.
S03_ARTEMISA_5: There's an old legend about the tree. They say that if the Great Root is restored to full power, it grants a gift â€” strength beyond mortal measure. Power enough to reshape the land itself.
S03_CHOICE_1A: Tell me more about this legend. What kind of power are we talking about?
S03_CHOICE_1B: Legends are just stories people tell when they're scared. What's actually happening to the tree?
S03_ARTEMISA_6A: Curious. Good. The legend says the Root was planted by Gaia herself, before even the Titans rose. Its power is primordial â€” older than the gods, older than memory.
S03_ARTEMISA_7A: If it could be healed, the energy released would be... immense. Maybe enough to fuel that ship of yours. Maybe enough to turn the tide against the darkness.
S03_ARTEMISA_6B: Fair enough. Legends don't stop chimera. But something is draining the tree from below â€” something in its roots. And that part isn't legend.
S03_ARTEMISA_7B: Whatever is killing it, we need to find out. And fast. Because when that tree dies, this village goes with it.
S03_NARRATOR_4: The tavern door creaks open. An old man enters â€” tall despite his years, leaning on a gnarled staff carved with symbols you don't recognize. The room falls respectfully silent.
S03_NARRATOR_5: His eyes are sharp beneath heavy brows, and when he speaks, even Artemis listens.
S03_LEADER_1: So. This is the stranger who fell from the sky. Artemis has told me about you.
S03_LEADER_2: I am Thalos, elder of Sardis. I've watched over this village for forty years. I've never seen things this bad.
S03_LEADER_3: But I bring news â€” troubling and hopeful in equal measure. Scouts have spotted Hippolyta in the northern mountains. The Amazon queen. She's alive.
S03_ARTEMISA_8: Hippolyta? Here? I thought she vanished after the fall of Themyscira.
S03_LEADER_4: She fights alone now. Hunts the monsters in the passes. My scouts say she's building something â€” fortifications, traps. Preparing for war.
S03_LEADER_5: If anyone knows what's happening to the Great Root, it may be her. The Amazons have guarded ancient knowledge for millennia. But reaching her... the mountain paths are treacherous.
S03_ARTEMISA_9: We'll find her. But first â€” Thalos, the tree. The temple at its base. The priestess there might know what's poisoning the roots.
S03_ARTEMISA_10: If we're going to war, we need to understand what we're fighting. And our new friend here could use a proper weapon. That dead metal thing won't cut it.
S03_NARRATOR_6: You leave the tavern and follow Artemis through the village toward the base of the Great Root. The trunk rises before you like a living mountain.
S03_NARRATOR_7: At its base, carved into the bark itself, is a doorway â€” ancient, ornate, framed by roots thick as castle walls. Bioluminescent moss lights the path inward.
S03_NARRATOR_8: Inside, the air thrums with power. You can feel it in your teeth, in the marrow of your bones. This place is old. Older than anything you've ever stood in.
S03_NARRATOR_9: A woman waits within â€” ageless, draped in robes of woven leaves and silver thread. Her eyes are closed, but she turns to face you as if she saw you coming long before you arrived.
S03_PRIESTESS_1: The tree told me you were coming. Both of you. It speaks in dreams now â€” faintly, like a voice underwater.
S03_PRIESTESS_2: The Great Root is poisoned. Deep below, its roots have reached something they should not have â€” the remains of a dead god, buried here since the first rebellion.
S03_PRIESTESS_3: The god's corpse leaks corruption. It rots the roots from within. The tree fights it, but it's losing. And when it falls, the barrier falls with it.
S03_ARTEMISA_11: A dead god... buried beneath the tree? How is that possible? Who buried it here?
S03_CHOICE_2A: Whatever you need from us, we'll do it. This tree must be saved.
S03_CHOICE_2B: A dead god's body is poisoning a magic tree. This world just keeps getting stranger.
S03_PRIESTESS_4A: Resolve. The tree responds to that. You may be from beyond the stars, but there is earth in your spirit.
S03_ARTEMISA_12A: Didn't expect that from you. Maybe there's more to the sky-traveler than crash landings and empty weapons.
S03_PRIESTESS_4B: Strange, yes. But real. And deadly. If you wish to survive this world, you must accept its truths â€” no matter how impossible they seem.
S03_ARTEMISA_12B: Welcome to my life. You get used to the impossible. Eventually.
S03_PRIESTESS_5: I cannot heal the tree alone. But I can offer you a gift. Step forward, and hold out your weapon.
S03_NARRATOR_10: The priestess raises her hands. The air shimmers. Emerald light pours from the walls of the temple, flowing like liquid into your pistol.
S03_NARRATOR_11: The dead weapon trembles, then hums. Green energy crackles along its barrel â€” alive, warm, pulsing with the heartbeat of the Great Root itself. It has ammunition now. Not bullets. Something older.
S03_ARTEMISA_13: Now that's more like it. A weapon blessed by the Root. Those chimera won't know what hit them.
S03_NARRATOR_12: You grip the transformed weapon. It feels different â€” heavier with purpose, alive with borrowed power. The priestess nods, and in her ancient eyes you see something rare: hope.
S04_NAR_01: The mountain trail narrows as you climb higher, the air thinning with every step. Ancient pines cling to the cliffs like skeletal fingers, and somewhere far below, a river roars through a gorge carved by millennia.
S04_NAR_02: Artemis walks ahead of you, sure-footed as a mountain goat, her silver hair catching the pale light that filters through the canopy.
S04_ART_01: You move well for someone who claims to be from... wherever it is you're from.
S04_ART_02: Most outsiders I've guided through these peaks couldn't keep up. You're different. I can feel it.
S04_ART_03: We've been walking together for three days now, and you've told me almost nothing about yourself. I shared my hunting grounds with you. The least you can offer is a story.
S04_C1A_OPEN: You deserve the truth. I came from beyond the sky â€” a space storm tore my vessel apart and cast me into your world.
S04_C1B_GUARDED: There isn't much to tell. I woke up in a crater with no memory of how I got here. That's the whole story.
S04_NAR_03: You tell her everything â€” the ship, the storm, the blinding light as the hull cracked open and the void swallowed you whole. Artemis listens without interrupting, her silver eyes wide.
S04_ART_04A: Beyond the sky... I've stared at the stars my entire life and never imagined someone could fall from them.
S04_ART_05A: You trusted me with something impossible. That means more than you know.
S04_NAR_04: You keep the details vague â€” a journey gone wrong, a crash you barely survived. Artemis studies your face, reading the half-truths like tracks in fresh snow.
S04_ART_04B: You're hiding something. I can always tell â€” the prey that holds still is the one with the most to lose.
S04_ART_05B: But I won't press. The mountains teach patience. When you're ready, I'll be here.
S04_ART_06: Since you shared something with me... I suppose it's only fair. There's something about myself I haven't told you.
S04_ART_07: My father is Zeus. Lord of the sky, king of the gods. I am Artemis, goddess of the hunt, of the wild places where no mortal dares tread.
S04_ART_08: I left Olympus when the Tree began to die. The other gods did nothing. I couldn't just watch the world unravel.
S04_C2A_AMAZED: A goddess... I'm walking with an actual goddess. That explains why even the wolves gave you a wide berth.
S04_C2B_SKEPTICAL: Daughter of Zeus. Right. And I suppose next you'll tell me that river down there was carved by Poseidon's trident.
S04_NAR_05: The ground shudders beneath your feet. A deep, tectonic groan echoes through the canyon as the cliff edge cracks and splinters. Artemis stumbles â€” her footing gives way over the waterfall's edge.
S04_NAR_06: She's falling. The waterfall drops two hundred feet into churning black water. You have one second to act.
S04_C3A_HEROIC: ARTEMIS! â€” You sprint to the edge and dive after her without hesitation, catching her arm mid-fall.
S04_C3B_INSTINCT: Your body moves before your mind catches up. You grab her wrist as the cliff crumbles beneath you both.
S04_NAR_07: You plunge together into the falls. The cold hits like a hammer blow â€” crushing, absolute. The current drags you down into a world of white foam and darkness.
S04_NAR_08: You surface gasping, still gripping Artemis. The river sweeps you both downstream until the current gentles near a rocky bank.
S04_ART_10: You... you jumped after me. You absolute fool. You wonderful, reckless fool.
S04_NAR_09: Soaked and shivering, you drag yourselves onto the bank. The waterfall roars behind you like a living thing. Through the trees, you spot the dark mouth of a cave.
S04_NAR_10: Inside the cave, you build a fire from dry brush. The flames throw dancing shadows across the stone walls. Artemis sits close, still dripping, her silver hair plastered against her neck.
S04_ART_11: Thank you. Genuinely. A goddess of the hunt, saved from a waterfall by a stranger who fell from the stars. The Fates have a cruel sense of humor.
S04_ART_12: I should be embarrassed. Instead I find myself... grateful. It's been a long time since anyone risked anything for me.
S04_NAR_11: The firelight catches something in her eyes â€” not gratitude alone, but something older and hungrier. She moves closer, and the space between you shrinks to nothing.
S04_ART_13: You're shivering. We both are. I know a way to fix that... if you're willing.
S04_ART_14: No titles in here. No gods and mortals. Just us, the fire, and whatever this is between us.
S05_NAR_01: A sound tears through the night â€” not thunder, but something worse. A deep, guttural howl that shakes the trees and sends birds screaming from the canopy.
S05_ART_01: That came from the village. Something is very wrong.
S05_NAR_02: You rush out of the cave together, the cold air biting your still-damp skin. The sky above the treeline glows an unnatural amber â€” the color of fire where fire should not be.
S05_NAR_03: The village is under siege. Twisted creatures â€” half-shadow, half-sinew â€” pour from rifts in the earth like black water from a broken dam. They move wrong, their limbs bending at angles that make your stomach turn.
S05_NAR_04: At the village center, the ancient tree that served as the settlement's ward flickers with dying light. Its bark cracks and peels, the protective barrier it sustained wavering like a candle in a gale.
S05_ART_02: The Tree's barrier is failing. Those creatures are drawn to it â€” they'll tear it apart and everyone inside with it.
S05_NAR_05: A cyclops â€” fifteen feet of muscle and fury â€” brings its fist down on a watchtower. The wood explodes into splinters. Villagers scatter like leaves.
S05_NAR_06: The barrier pulses once, twice, then a section shatters like glass. The creatures surge through the gap, howling.
S05_ART_04: The barrier is breached! If we don't stop that cyclops, the whole village falls!
S05_NAR_07: Then you see her. A woman with hair like forge-fire â€” crimson and wild â€” is already fighting the cyclops alone. She moves with savage precision, her spear a blur of bronze as she drives it into the beast's knee.
S05_NAR_08: The cyclops roars and swings. She rolls under the blow, coming up with blood on her face and a grin that could cut steel.
S05_ART_05: That woman... she's holding it alone. She won't last long without help. Let's go!
S05_NAR_09: You charge into the fray beside Artemis, weapons drawn, as the cyclops turns its one burning eye toward you.
S05_NAR_10: The cyclops crashes to the earth, its final breath a wet gurgle that shakes the ground. Dust and ash settle over the ruined village square. The red-haired woman plants her spear in the dirt and turns to face you both.
S05_HIPO_01: Not bad. You actually know which end of a sword to hold. That puts you ahead of most men I've met.
S05_HIPO_02: Name's Hippolyta. Queen of the Amazons. Or what's left of them.
S05_ART_06: I'm Artemis. And this is my companion. We saw the fire from the mountains.
S05_HIPO_03: Artemis. The huntress. I've heard stories â€” the goddess who walks among mortals instead of lounging on Olympus with the others.
S05_HIPO_04: At least one god has the spine to get her hands dirty.
S05_NAR_11: Hippolyta wipes the cyclops blood from her cheek and surveys the damage. The village smolders behind her â€” homes reduced to char, the ancient tree's barrier still flickering weakly.
S05_HIPO_05: These attacks have been getting worse. Every week, more rifts open. More creatures pour through. The tree is dying â€” and when it goes, this entire region goes dark.
S05_HIPO_06: I've lost warriors trying to hold the line. Good women. I can't do this alone anymore.
S05_ART_07: We're heading toward the source of the corruption. If you want to stop the rifts, you could join us.
S05_HIPO_07: I don't join. I lead. But... I'll walk beside you. For now. Until I decide whether you're worth following into the dark.
S06_NAR_01: The tavern is a low-ceilinged den of smoke and amber light, built into the roots of an enormous dead oak. It smells of pine resin, spilled mead, and something faintly metallic â€” old blood, perhaps, soaked into the floorboards.
S06_NAR_02: Artemis sits in the corner nursing a cup of mountain tea. Hippolyta stands at the bar, arms crossed, drawing looks from every patron in the room â€” none of whom dare hold her gaze.
S06_HIPO_01: So. We're going to talk about the monsters and the tree and whatever ancient evil is rotting this world from the inside out. Fine. But first â€” I need to know what I'm working with.
S06_HIPO_02: I mean that I've seen a hundred so-called heroes walk into the dark and not come back. Big swords, bigger promises, and nothing between their ears but air.
S06_HIPO_03: The rifts are connected to something deep â€” something that predates the gods. The tree's roots reach into the underworld itself. Whatever is killing it lives down there.
S06_ART_02: The corruption runs deeper than I feared. If the roots are compromised, Olympus itself could fall.
S06_HIPO_04: Olympus can burn for all I care. I'm here for my people. But if stopping this rot means fighting beside a goddess and a sky-stranger... then I need proof you can fight.
S06_HIPO_05: I'll help you. I'll bring my Amazons, my weapons, everything I have. But only if your friend here can beat me in single combat.
S06_HIPO_06: Don't look so worried, huntress. I won't break your toy. Much.
S06_C1A_CONFIDENT: No, let her have this. I've faced worse than an Amazon queen. Let's dance, Hippolyta.
S06_C1B_RELUCTANT: I'd rather not fight an ally. But if that's what it takes to earn your trust... then I accept.
S06_HIPO_07A: Ha! THAT is what I wanted to hear. Fire in the belly. I was starting to think the huntress kept you around for your looks.
S06_HIPO_08A: Outside. Now. And don't hold back â€” I'll know if you do, and I'll make you regret it.
S06_HIPO_07B: Reluctant? Good. Only fools rush into a fight with me eagerly. The wise ones hesitate â€” and then commit.
S06_HIPO_08B: But make no mistake. Once we step outside, I won't hold back. Earn my respect or lose my interest.
S06_NAR_03: Hippolyta kicks the tavern door open and strides into the moonlit yard, rolling her shoulders. Artemis catches your arm as you pass.
S06_NAR_04: Hippolyta is on her knees, breathing hard, her spear snapped in two beside her. Blood runs from a cut above her eye, and for the first time since you met her, she is smiling â€” truly smiling.
S06_HIPO_09: No man has ever beaten me. Not once. Not in twenty years of war across three continents.
S06_HIPO_10: You... interest me. More than interest me. I haven't felt this alive since I killed my first hydra.
S06_HIPO_11: The night is young, and I have a room upstairs. You earned more than my alliance tonight. Come with me.
S06_C2B_SURPRISED: I didn't expect this. But I'd be a fool to say no to an Amazon queen.
S06_HIPO_12A: I like a man who knows when to follow. Don't worry â€” I'll be gentle. At first.
S06_HIPO_12B: Surprised? Get used to it. I take what I want, and right now... I want you.
S06_NAR_05: Hippolyta takes your hand â€” her grip iron-strong and warm â€” and leads you toward the stairs. Artemis watches from the corner, her silver eyes unreadable, a faint smile on her lips.
S06_HIPO_13: Close the door behind you. And forget the word 'gentle.'
S07_NAR_01: The village square is crowded. Torches flicker against weathered stone as the council gathers beneath the ancient oak.
S07_NAR_02: Artemis and Hippolyta stand at your side. The village leader steps forward, his face carved deep with worry.
S07_VL_01: Thank you all for coming. I wish I bore better tidings.
S07_VL_02: Our scouts returned at dawn. What they found in the mountains... it changes everything.
S07_VL_03: The creatures are no longer just raiding the outskirts. They're organized. Moving in formation, pressing closer every night.
S07_VL_04: And they're getting stronger. The beasts we drove off last season would be nothing compared to what stalks those passes now.
S07_ART_01: Organized? That's not natural behavior for mountain creatures. Something is directing them.
S07_HIPO_01: How many? Give me numbers, not fears.
S07_VL_05: Dozens, maybe more. But that's not the worst of it. Near the old ruins north of the pass, the scouts found something.
S07_VL_06: A crystal. Dark as a moonless night, half-buried in the earth. The ground around it was dead -- no grass, no insects, nothing. And the creatures... they gather around it like moths to a flame.
S07_ART_02: A dark crystal near the ruins... This reeks of old magic. The kind that should have stayed buried.
S07_HIPO_02: Then we dig it out and shatter it. If that's what's drawing them, we cut the head off the snake.
S07_NAR_03: A heavy silence settles over the square. The villagers exchange uneasy glances. You can feel the fear in the air, thick as woodsmoke.
S07_ART_03: We need to prepare. If those creatures are massing around that crystal, a siege could come any day.
S07_HIPO_03: Agreed. Sharpen every blade in this village. We'll need them all before this is over.
S07_NAR_04: The council disperses into the torchlit dark. Somewhere in the mountains, something ancient and hungry stirs.
S08_NAR_01: Three days pass. Three days of sharpening steel, reinforcing walls, and sleeping in shifts.
S08_NAR_02: On the third night, you are woken not by dawn, but by the scream of a war horn splitting the darkness.
S08_NAR_03: The village erupts into chaos. Shadows pour from the treeline -- dozens of twisted shapes, eyes burning with unnatural light.
S08_ART_01: They're here! Everyone to the barricades, NOW!
S08_HIPO_01: Finally. I was getting tired of waiting.
S08_NAR_04: Steel clashes against claw and fang. The village defenders hold the line, but the creatures keep coming, wave after wave, driven by something beyond hunger.
S08_ART_02: We can hold them here, but this won't end until that crystal is destroyed. Someone has to reach the enemy camp.
S08_HIPO_02: Artemis and I will keep these beasts busy. You need to get to that crystal.
S08_ART_03: The ruins are north of the pass. How you get there is up to you.
S08_C1A_STEALTH: I'll move through the shadows. They won't see me coming.
S08_C1B_DIRECT: I'll cut straight through them. Speed over stealth.
S08_ART_04: Smart. Use the creek bed -- it runs past their flank. Stay low and stay quiet.
S08_NAR_05A: You slip into the night, keeping to the shadows, moving through the dry creek bed that winds toward the mountain pass. The sounds of battle fade behind you.
S08_NAR_06A: The enemy camp appears through the trees -- a clearing lit by sickly green light. Most of the creatures have joined the assault on the village. The path to the crystal is nearly clear.
S08_HIPO_03: Ha! Now that's a warrior's answer. Run fast, hit hard, don't look back.
S08_NAR_05B: You charge into the darkness, blade drawn, cutting through the scattered beasts that stand between you and the ruins. Adrenaline burns through your veins like liquid fire.
S08_NAR_06B: You break through the last line of creatures and stumble into the enemy camp -- a clearing bathed in unnatural green light. The crystal is close. You can feel it pulling at something inside you.
S08_NAR_07: The dark crystal rises from the center of the ruins like a black tooth jutting from the earth. It pulses with slow, rhythmic light -- a heartbeat of corrupted energy.
S08_NAR_08: The air around it is thick, almost liquid. Each breath tastes of iron and ash. Dead vines cling to the stone floor, withered by whatever poison the crystal bleeds into the ground.
S08_NAR_09: As you approach, the crystal flares. The ground cracks. Something massive begins to take shape in the dark energy pouring from its surface -- a spirit of earth and fury, ancient beyond reckoning.
S08_NAR_10: Gaia's wrath given form. It will not let you destroy what remains of her power.
S08_NAR_11: The spirit howls, and the ruins tremble. There is no running from this. You draw your weapon.
S08_NAR_13: The spirit shatters. The crystal cracks, then explodes in a cascade of dark shards that dissolve into the wind like black snow.
S08_NAR_14: In the distance, you hear the creatures shriek -- confused, directionless. The invisible leash that drove them has been severed. You make your way back through the pass as dawn breaks over the mountains.
S08_ART_05: You did it! The creatures scattered the moment the crystal was destroyed. I knew you could do it.
S08_HIPO_04: Not bad at all. You've got the heart of a real warrior in there somewhere.
S08_NAR_15: By midday, the village has transformed. What was a battlefield hours ago is now a celebration ground. Fires crackle, meat roasts on spits, and someone has produced wine from a cellar no one knew existed.
S08_NAR_16: The villagers raise their cups to you. Children reenact the battle with wooden swords. For the first time since you crashed into this world, you feel like you belong.
S08_ART_06: You know, for someone who fell out of the sky, you've become quite the local hero. How does it feel?
S08_HIPO_05: Drink up, crystal-breaker. You've earned every drop tonight.
S08_C2A_HUMBLE: I just did what anyone would have done. The real heroes are the ones who held the line.
S08_C2B_PROUD: It feels good. That spirit didn't know what hit it.
S08_NAR_17: The celebration stretches deep into the night. Laughter and song fill the air, pushing back the darkness that tried to claim this place.
S08_ART_07: Tonight we celebrate. But tomorrow... there's still so much we don't understand about those crystals. This isn't over.
S08_HIPO_06: One battle down. Something tells me there are plenty more where that came from. But tonight? Tonight is ours.
S08_NAR_18: You lean back and watch the sparks from the bonfire drift upward into the star-filled sky. The siege is over. But somewhere out there, in the dark places of this world, other crystals still pulse with ancient power.
S09_NAR_01: Morning light filters through the canopy as you return to the tree temple. The ancient tree stands taller now, its bark no longer ashen, though faint dark veins still trace its trunk like old scars.
S09_NAR_02: The priestess is waiting. She sits at the base of the tree, translucent as morning mist, her form flickering gently between solid and ethereal.
S09_PRI_01: You came back. I felt the crystal shatter last night... felt the corruption lift from the roots of this tree like poison drawn from a wound.
S09_PRI_02: You have my gratitude, wanderer. More than you know.
S09_PRI_03: That crystal you destroyed was no ordinary stone. It was a fragment of Gaia -- the Earth Mother herself.
S09_PRI_04: When Gaia fell, her essence fractured into three pieces. Each one carries a portion of her authority over the living world. The crystal in the ruins was the first -- the Fragment of Corruption.
S09_PRI_05: It had been twisting the land for centuries, turning creatures into weapons, poisoning the roots of this sacred tree. Without you, it would have consumed everything.
S09_NAR_03: The priestess rises to her feet. Her form solidifies for a moment, and you see her clearly -- ancient eyes in a young face, beauty that belongs to another age entirely.
S09_PRI_06: There is something else you must know. The second fragment...
S09_NAR_04: Her words hang in the air. The light around her shifts, and for an instant you see it -- a faint emerald glow pulsing beneath her skin, the same rhythmic heartbeat as the dark crystal, but warm. Alive.
S09_PRI_08: I am the Fragment of Memory. Gaia's consciousness given form. That is why only you can see me -- your soul carries something from beyond this world, something that resonates with the fragments. No one born of this earth can perceive me.
S09_C1A_DETERMINED: Then I'll find the third fragment. Whatever it takes.
S09_C1B_OVERWHELMED: This is... a lot to take in. Gaia, fragments, a power I don't understand...
S09_PRI_09A: That fire in your eyes... yes. You are the one the earth has been waiting for.
S09_PRI_10A: Hold onto that resolve. You will need every shred of it for what lies ahead.
S09_PRI_09B: I know. The weight of it is heavy. But you do not have to carry it alone.
S09_PRI_10B: You have already proven your strength. Trust yourself, as I trust you.
S09_PRI_11: The third fragment -- the Fragment of Authority -- is in the hands of a sorceress in the city of Thebes. She has bent its power to her own will, and the land around her city suffers for it.
S09_PRI_12: If you can claim all three fragments, you will hold Gaia's full authority. Enough power to restore the sacred tree... and perhaps to heal this broken world.
S09_NAR_05: The path is clear now. Thebes. The sorceress. The final fragment. But for this moment, the temple is quiet, and the priestess stands close.
S09_PRI_13: Before you go... let me thank you properly.
S09_NAR_06: She steps closer. The air grows warm, heavy with the scent of wildflowers and ancient earth. Her fingertips brush your hand, and where she touches, your skin hums with gentle, golden light.
S09_PRI_14: I have been alone for so very long. Centuries of silence, watching the world decay, unable to touch anyone, unable to be seen. And then you arrived.
S09_PRI_15: Stay with me. Just for a little while. Let me remember what it feels like... to not be alone.
S09_NAR_07: The light of the temple dims to a soft, amber glow. The ancient tree shelters you both beneath its newly healed branches, and the world beyond these walls grows very, very quiet.
S09_SED_1: You've earned more than words can say. Let me show you my gratitude... the way only a goddess can.
S09_SED_2: The priestess's tunic slips from one shoulder. Her skin glows with a faint, golden light â€” the warmth of the earth itself radiating from within her.
S09_SED_3: These hands have held the roots of the world together for centuries. Tonight, they hold only you.
S09_SED_4: She kneels before you, her blonde hair cascading over her shoulders. The amber light of the temple paints her curves in warm gold.
S09_SED_5: Let me worship you... the way mortals once worshipped me.
S09_SED_6: The temple fills with the sound of soft breath and the rustle of sacred cloth falling to the stone floor. The great tree above seems to pulse in rhythm.
S09_SED_7: Mmm... your energy... it's like the sun after an endless winter...
S09_SED_8: Time loses meaning beneath the ancient branches. The priestess gives herself completely â€” every touch carrying the weight of centuries of loneliness finally broken.
S09_SED_9: Thank you... truly. You've given me something no fragment of Gaia could ever provide.
S09_SED_10: As dawn breaks through the temple canopy, the priestess rests against you, her golden glow dimming to a peaceful warmth. The journey to Thebes awaits, but for now, this moment belongs only to you both.
PROLOGUE_LINE_02: Heroes and Gods won the war against Zeus, Poseidon, and Hades.
PROLOGUE_LINE_03: The rebellion was led by Heracles, Mnemosyne, and a hero from another world.
PROLOGUE_LINE_04: Peace lasted a thousand years. Until the Titans woke.
PROLOGUE_LINE_05: Kronos, father of Zeus, used his authority over time to return to the climax of the rebellion.
PROLOGUE_LINE_06: With both sides weakened, he killed Zeus, Poseidon, and the hero.
PROLOGUE_LINE_07: Now the world is broken. Gods scattered. Their authority lost.
PROLOGUE_LINE_08: Sardis. Present day. You wake in a forest, your head pounding.
PROLOGUE_LINE_09: Your ship lies broken nearby — hull cracked, power core leaking. The mission is over before it began.
PROLOGUE_LINE_10: You pull your pistol from its holster. Still working. The targeting HUD flickers but holds.
PROLOGUE_LINE_11_SHAKE: Branches snap. Twisted creatures pour from the treeline — eyes glowing with stolen divine light.
PROLOGUE_LINE_12: No time to think. They're already on you.
CRASH_RESCUE_01: You drop the last one, chest heaving. The targeting HUD flashes amber — power reserves critical.
CRASH_RESCUE_02_SHAKE: More shapes move in the trees. A dozen. A hundred. You fire until the HUD goes red. [CLICK] — empty.
CRASH_RESCUE_03: A silver arrow whistles past your ear and pins the nearest creature to a tree.
CRASH_RESCUE_04: A woman with a glowing bow leaps from the shadows — she fires three more shots before you can blink. The rest of the creatures flee into the dark.
CRASH_RESCUE_05_SHAKE: You try to thank her. Your vision blurs. The forest tilts sideways. You fall.
CH01_BLESSING_01: The temple at the base of the Great Root is older than memory. The air inside hums. The walls breathe.
CH01_BLESSING_02: The tree told me you were coming. It speaks in dreams now — faintly, like a voice underwater.
CH01_BLESSING_03: The Great Root is dying. Its roots have reached the remains of a dead god, buried here since the first rebellion. The corpse leaks corruption.
CH01_BLESSING_04: You are not of this earth. I can see it in the weave of your soul. That makes you the only one who can carry what I must give.
CH01_BLESSING_05: Wait — what exactly are you giving him?
CH01_BLESSING_06: A fragment of the Root's last light. Step forward, stranger. Hold out your weapon.
CH01_BLESSING_07: The energy you seek to restore your ship — it exists here, in older forms. Take this first.
CH01_BLESSING_08_SHAKE: Emerald light pours from the temple walls and flows into your pistol. The HUD flares green — ammo counter climbing from 0 to FULL in seconds.
CH01_BLESSING_09: The weapon hums with a new rhythm — alive, pulsing in time with the Great Root's heartbeat.
CH01_BLESSING_10: By all the gods... I've never seen the priestess give her light to anyone. Let's go — Hippolyta won't wait.
