CHAPTER_1_LOCATION: The ancient kingdom of Sardis lies at the edge of the mortal world. Once a seat of kings, now a refuge for scattered gods.
CH01_N01_LINE_01: You wake in a forest. Your ship is gone. A girl with a bow stands over a dead monster — and over you.
CH01_N01_LINE_02: You can stop pointing that dead weapon at the trees. They're gone.
CH01_N01_LINE_03: You're alive. That's already more than most people manage in this forest.
CH01_N01_LINE_04: She steps out of the shadows — sharp-eyed, silver-haired, a bow still glowing faintly in her grip.
CH01_N01_LINE_05: I'm Artemis. Huntress. And you just survived your first five minutes in Sardis.
CH01_N01_LINE_06: Your clothes aren't from any land I know. Neither is that weapon you were waving around.
CH01_N01_LINE_07: A space storm. Your ship crashed. You're a long way from home.
CH01_N01_LINE_08: The forest is no place for a wounded stranger. Come — I'll take you somewhere safe.
CH01_N01_LINE_09: You follow the huntress through the dark forest. Behind you, the wreckage of your ship burns against the stars.
CH01_N01_CHOICE_A: My ship crashed. I need to find my power core.
CH01_N01_CHOICE_B: Where am I? What were those things?
CH01_N01_RESPONSE_A: My ship went down in a space storm. If I don't find the power core, I'm stranded here.
CH01_N01_RESPONSE_B: Those creatures — what were they? And where exactly have I landed?
CH01_N02_LINE_01: You wake in a real bed. Through the window, an enormous tree — bigger than any you've ever seen — rises into the clouds.
CH01_N02_LINE_02: Finally awake. I was starting to think you'd sleep through the whole apocalypse.
CH01_N02_LINE_03: This is the magical forest of Sardis. That tree out there is the Great Root — it's what keeps this village alive.
CH01_N02_LINE_04: Those creatures you met? They're getting stronger. If we don't act, they'll siege the village within the month.
CH01_N02_LINE_05: I'll help you find the energy your ship needs. But I need a fighter at my side — the forest is no longer safe alone.
CH01_N02_LINE_06: A deal, then. You help me defend this place, and I guide you to what your ship needs. Fair?
CH01_N02_LINE_07: Good. Come on — I want you to meet the village before the next wave hits.
CH01_N02_LINE_08: Careful and cautious. I can work with that. Most people who rush into these forests don't come back.
CH01_N02_PROMPT_A: Will you help me defend the village?
CH01_N02_PROMPT_B: What exactly am I agreeing to fight?
CH01_N02_CHOICE_A1: You've got a deal. Point me at the monsters.
CH01_N02_CHOICE_A2: I'm in. Tell me everything about the threat.
CH01_N02_CHOICE_B1: What are these creatures, and why are they attacking?
CH01_N02_CHOICE_B2: How bad is it, really? I need to understand what I'm walking into.
CH01_N02_RESPONSE_A1: Deal. Point me at the monsters and let's get started.
CH01_N02_RESPONSE_A2: I'm in — but I want the full picture before we move.
CH01_N02_RESPONSE_B1: Tell me what these things are and why they're here.
CH01_N02_RESPONSE_B2: I need to understand the scale of this before I commit.
CH01_N02_LINE_09: You'd charge into a war you barely understand? Either you're very brave or very stupid.
CH01_N02_LINE_10: Either way — welcome to Sardis. Try not to die on the first day.
CH01_N02_LINE_11: Cautious. Good. The reckless ones are always the first to fall in these woods.
CH01_N03_LINE_01: The tavern is warm and low-lit, filled with the kind of quiet that comes from people who are afraid to speak too loudly.
CH01_N03_LINE_02: The Great Root channels ancient power — but it's weakening. Holes are opening in the barrier it creates.
CH01_N03_LINE_03: Every week the monsters press closer. Last week they reached the eastern gate. People have died.
CH01_N03_LINE_04: There's an old legend. They say if the Great Root is restored, it grants power enough to reshape the land itself.
CH01_N03_LINE_05: The legend says the Root was planted by Gaia — older than the gods, older than memory. If it heals, the energy released could be immense.
CH01_N03_LINE_06: Maybe enough to fuel that ship of yours.
CH01_N03_LINE_07: I am Thalos, elder of Sardis. I've never seen things this bad — and I have news that is both troubling and hopeful.
CH01_N03_LINE_08: Scouts have spotted Hippolyta in the northern mountains. The Amazon queen is alive — and she's preparing for war.
CH01_N03_LINE_09: We'll find her. But first — the temple at the Root's base. The priestess there may know what's poisoning the roots.
CH01_N03_LINE_10: If we're going to war, we need to understand what we're fighting. Come — the temple is this way.
CH01_N03_LINE_11: The priestess waits within, ageless, robed in leaves and silver thread. She turns before you reach her — as if she knew you were coming.
CH01_N03_LINE_12: The tree told me you were coming. It speaks in dreams now — faintly, like a voice underwater.
CH01_N03_LINE_13: The Great Root is poisoned. Its roots have reached the remains of a dead god, buried here since the first rebellion. The corpse leaks corruption.
CH01_N03_LINE_14: Step forward. Hold out your weapon — let me give you something to fight with.
CH01_N03_LINE_15: Emerald light pours from the temple walls and flows into your pistol. It hums — alive now, pulsing with the heartbeat of the Root.
CH01_N03_PROMPT: The priestess offers her blessing. Do you accept?
CH01_N03_CHOICE_A: Whatever you need from us — we'll do it. This tree must be saved.
CH01_N03_CHOICE_B: A dead god's corpse is poisoning a magic tree. This world just keeps getting stranger.
CH01_N03_RESPONSE_A: Tell us what to do. We'll see it through.
CH01_N03_RESPONSE_B: Strange — but real. And deadly. Point us toward the threat.
CH01_N04_LINE_01: The mountain trail narrows as you climb. Ancient pines cling to the cliffs, and the air thins with every step.
CH01_N04_LINE_02: You move well for someone who fell out of the sky. Most outsiders I've guided through these peaks couldn't keep up.
CH01_N04_LINE_03: We've been walking together for days and you've told me almost nothing. I shared my hunting grounds with you — the least you can offer is a story.
CH01_N04_LINE_04: My father is Zeus. Lord of the sky. I am Artemis, goddess of the hunt, daughter of a dead king.
CH01_N04_LINE_05: I left Olympus when the Root began to die. The other gods did nothing. I couldn't watch the world unravel.
CH01_N04_LINE_06: Beyond the sky... I've stared at the stars my whole life and never imagined someone could fall from them.
CH01_N04_LINE_07: You trusted me with something impossible. That means more than you know.
CH01_N04_LINE_08: The ground shudders. The cliff edge cracks — and Artemis falls toward the waterfall below.
CH01_N04_LINE_09: You jump. The cold hits like a hammer blow — crushing, total — and you surface still gripping her arm.
CH01_N04_LINE_10: You absolute fool. You wonderful, reckless fool.
CH01_N04_LINE_11: Inside the cave, a fire. Artemis sits close, dripping, her silver hair plastered against her neck.
CH01_N04_LINE_12: Thank you. A goddess of the hunt, saved by a stranger from the stars. The Fates have a cruel sense of humor.
CH01_N04_LINE_13: A sound tears through the night — not thunder. Something worse. A guttural howl that shakes the trees.
CH01_N04_LINE_14: That came from the village. Something is very wrong — we have to move.
CH01_N04_PROMPT_A: She tells you about Zeus. How do you respond?
CH01_N04_PROMPT_B: You've just pulled her from a waterfall. Now what?
CH01_N04_CHOICE_A1: A goddess... walking with me through these mountains. That explains a lot.
CH01_N04_CHOICE_A2: Daughter of Zeus — and you're here, in this forest, fighting monsters alone.
CH01_N04_CHOICE_B1: Are you hurt? We need to get out of the water.
CH01_N04_CHOICE_B2: The village. Something's wrong — I can hear it from here.
CH01_N04_RESPONSE_A1: A goddess. That explains why even the wolves gave you a wide berth.
CH01_N04_RESPONSE_A2: Daughter of Zeus, and you're here protecting one village. That says everything about you.
CH01_N04_RESPONSE_B1: Are you hurt? We need to move — that current nearly took us both.
CH01_N04_RESPONSE_B2: We need to get back. That howl came from the direction of the village.
CH01_N05_LINE_01: The sky above the village glows amber — fire where fire should not be. Twisted creatures pour from rifts in the earth like black water.
CH01_N05_LINE_02: The Great Root's barrier is full of holes. Those things are drawn to it — they'll tear it apart and everyone with it.
CH01_N05_LINE_03: A cyclops — fifteen feet of muscle and fury — brings its fist down on the watchtower. The wood explodes into splinters.
CH01_N05_LINE_04: Then you see her. A woman with hair like forge-fire — crimson, wild — already fighting the cyclops alone, her spear a blur of bronze.
CH01_N05_LINE_05: The cyclops swings. She rolls under the blow, comes up with blood on her face and a grin that could cut steel.
CH01_N05_LINE_06: She won't last long without help. Move!
CH01_N05_LINE_07: The cyclops crashes to earth, its final breath shaking the ground. The red-haired woman plants her spear and turns to face you.
CH01_N05_LINE_08: Not bad. You actually know which end of a sword to hold — that puts you ahead of most men I've met.
CH01_N05_LINE_09: I'm Hippolyta. Queen of the Amazons. Or what's left of them.
CH01_N05_CHOICE_A: We saw the fire from the mountains. We came as fast as we could.
CH01_N05_CHOICE_B: That cyclops would have flattened this place without you.
CH01_N05_RESPONSE_A: We saw the fire from the mountains and ran. You've been holding this alone?
CH01_N05_RESPONSE_B: You held it off single-handed. That cyclops would have leveled the whole village.
CH01_N06_LINE_01: I'm Artemis. This is my companion. We watched you fight — you move like you've done it a thousand times.
CH01_N06_LINE_02: Artemis. The huntress. At least one god has the spine to get her hands dirty.
CH01_N06_LINE_03: These attacks have been getting worse — every week, more rifts, more creatures. I've lost warriors holding the line.
CH01_N06_LINE_04: I can't do this alone anymore. But I don't join causes — I lead them. Convince me you're worth following.
CH01_N06_LINE_05: We're heading for the source of the corruption. Join us and we stop the rifts for good.
CH01_N06_LINE_06: I don't join. I walk beside people who've proven themselves. Can your companion prove it?
CH01_N06_CHOICE_A: We saw you fight. Come with us — together we end this.
CH01_N06_CHOICE_B: What would it take for you to trust us?
CH01_N06_RESPONSE_A: You've already been fighting this war alone. Come with us and we finish it together.
CH01_N06_RESPONSE_B: Tell us what you need to see. We'll show you.
CH01_N06_LINE_07: Conditions? Ha! At least you're not just nodding along like every other fool who's crossed my path.
CH01_N06_LINE_08: The duel begins. Hippolyta moves like wildfire — fast, relentless, every strike a test.
CH01_N06_LINE_09: You held your ground. That's rare. I've broken warriors twice your size in half the time.
CH01_N06_LINE_10: Hippolyta sheathes her spear. For the first time, she looks at you not as an outsider — but as an equal.
CH01_N07_LINE_01: I don't follow anyone I haven't tested. That's not pride — that's strategy.
CH01_N07_LINE_02: Fight me. Prove you can stand your ground, and I'll walk beside you all the way to the source.
CH01_N07_LINE_03: No man has beaten me in single combat. Not once. Not in twenty years of war across three continents.
CH01_N07_LINE_04: She's breathing hard, spear snapped in two beside her — and for the first time since you met her, she's smiling.
CH01_N07_LINE_05: You... interest me. More than interest me. I haven't felt this alive since I killed my first hydra.
CH01_N07_LINE_06: You've earned my alliance. And my respect — which is harder to earn.
CH01_N07_CHOICE_A: Challenge accepted. I won't make it easy.
CH01_N07_CHOICE_B: I'll fight — first blood only. No broken bones.
CH01_N07_RESPONSE_A: Accepted. Don't hold back — I'll know if you do.
CH01_N07_RESPONSE_B: I'll fight. But let's agree — first blood, nothing permanent.
CH01_N08_LINE_01: The village square fills with torchlight as the council gathers. Thalos steps forward, his face carved deep with worry.
CH01_N08_LINE_02: Our scouts returned at dawn. The creatures are no longer just raiding the outskirts — they're organized, moving in formation.
CH01_N08_LINE_03: And they're getting stronger. The beasts we drove off last season are nothing compared to what stalks those passes now.
CH01_N08_LINE_04: Organized movement — something is directing them. That's not natural behavior.
CH01_N08_LINE_05: Near the old ruins north of the pass, the scouts found something. A crystal — dark as a moonless night, half-buried in the earth.
CH01_N08_LINE_06: The ground around it was dead — no grass, no insects. And the creatures gather around it like moths to a flame.
CH01_N07_LINE_07: Then we march at dawn. I'll bring every Amazon I have left — and gods help whatever stands in our way.
CH01_N07_LINE_08: Together, then. All of us. Whatever waits at those ruins won't expect three forces united.
CH01_N08_LINE_07: A dark crystal near ruins where monsters cluster. That's our target. We find it and shatter it.
CH01_N08_LINE_08: We need to prepare. If those creatures are massing around that crystal, a siege could come any day.
CH01_N08_LINE_09: Agreed. Sharpen every blade in this village. We'll need them all before this is over.
CH01_N09_LINE_01: Three days pass — sharpening steel, reinforcing walls, sleeping in shifts.
CH01_N09_LINE_02: On the third night, a war horn tears the dark apart. The siege has begun.
CH01_N09_LINE_04: Finally. I was getting tired of waiting.
CH01_N09_LINE_05: We hold them here — but this won't end until that crystal is destroyed. Someone has to reach the enemy camp.
CH01_N09_LINE_06: Artemis and I keep these beasts busy. You get to that crystal.
CH01_N09_LINE_07: The dark crystal rises from the ruins like a black tooth. It pulses with slow, rhythmic light — a heartbeat of corrupted energy.
CH01_N09_LINE_08: As you approach, the crystal flares. The ground cracks. Something massive takes shape in the dark energy — ancient, vast, furious.
CH01_N09_LINE_09: Gaia's wrath given form. It will not let you destroy what remains of her power.
CH01_N09_LINE_10: The spirit shatters. The crystal explodes in dark shards that dissolve like black snow.
CH01_N09_LINE_11: You did it — the creatures scattered the moment the crystal broke. I knew you could do it.
CH01_N09_LINE_12: Not bad at all. You've got the heart of a real warrior in there somewhere.
CH01_N09_CHOICE_A: I'll move through the shadows — they won't see me coming.
CH01_N09_CHOICE_B: I'll cut straight through them. Speed over stealth.
CH01_N09_PROMPT: The fragments of a dead goddess. Three pieces scattered across the world. What will you do with this knowledge?
CH01_N09_LINE_13: The sun rises over Sardis. The sacred tree stands taller. The first fragment is claimed — but two more remain, and the road ahead is longer than you know.
CH01_N09_LINE_14: Doubt is wise. But the fragments will not wait for certainty. They call to those who can hear them — and you hear them clearly.
CH01_N09_RESPONSE_A: I'll go quiet — through the shadows, around their flank.
CH01_N09_RESPONSE_B: I'll go straight through. Speed is the only stealth I need.
CH1_NARRATOR_01: The forest stirs around you. Something watches from the shadows.
CH1_ARTEMISA_01: Stay close. These woods have teeth.
CH01_N10_LINE_01: The tree stands taller in the morning light — bark no longer ashen, though faint dark veins still trace its trunk like old scars.
CH01_N10_LINE_02: The priestess waits at the temple. Her form flickers between solid and ethereal — translucent as morning mist.
CH01_N10_LINE_03: I felt the crystal shatter last night. Felt the corruption lift from the roots like poison drawn from a wound.
CH01_N10_LINE_04: That crystal was no ordinary stone. It was a fragment of Gaia — the Earth Mother herself.
CH01_N10_LINE_05: When Gaia fell, her essence fractured into three pieces. Each carries a portion of her authority over the living world.
CH01_N10_LINE_06: The crystal in the ruins was the first fragment — the Fragment of Corruption. Without you, it would have consumed everything.
CH01_N10_LINE_07: There is something else you must know. The second fragment...
CH01_N10_LINE_09: A faint emerald glow pulses beneath her skin — the same rhythm as the dark crystal, but warm. Alive.
CH01_N10_LINE_10: I am the Fragment of Memory. Gaia's consciousness given form. That is why only you can see me.
CH01_N10_LINE_11: Your soul carries something from beyond this world — something that resonates with the fragments. No one born of this earth can perceive me.
CH01_N10_LINE_12: The third fragment — the Fragment of Authority — is with a sorceress in the city of Thebes. She has bent its power to her own will.
CH01_N10_LINE_13: Claim all three fragments and you will hold Gaia's full authority. Enough power to restore the sacred tree — and perhaps to heal this broken world.
CH01_N10_CHOICE_A: Then I'll find the third fragment. Whatever it takes.
CH01_N10_CHOICE_B: This is a lot to carry. But I understand what's at stake.
CH01_N10_RESPONSE_A: I'll find the third fragment. Tell me everything you know about Thebes.
CH01_N10_RESPONSE_B: Gaia's authority, three fragments, a sorceress in Thebes. I understand — let's move.
